access.guest_fallback_message: Detailed explanations are available for logged-in Saola students only.

q33.explanation: Câu 33 yêu cầu nối thông tin trong câu hỏi với một lựa chọn. Đoạn B chứa căn cứ: "Since the turn of the 21st century, rationality has experienced a rising popularity and several devoted online communities have formed to champion and teach rational thinking. Perhaps the most notable of these is Less Wrong, a community started in 2006 by artificial intelligence researcher Eliezer Yudkowsky and economist Robin Hanson. Any community member can participate by attending regular 'physical' meetups in different cities around the globe or spark debate by posting on its website or forum.". Lựa chọn C (theo bảng đáp án) là lựa chọn khớp với chi tiết này.
q33.evidence: Since the turn of the 21st century, rationality has experienced a rising popularity and several devoted online communities have formed to champion and teach rational thinking. Perhaps the most notable of these is Less Wrong, a community started in 2006 by artificial intelligence researcher Eliezer Yudkowsky and economist Robin Hanson. Any community member can participate by attending regular 'physical' meetups in different cities around the globe or spark debate by posting on its website or forum._

q34.explanation: Câu 34 yêu cầu nối thông tin trong câu hỏi với một lựa chọn. Đoạn D chứa căn cứ: "Through this system on its website and forum, Less Wrong community members debate concepts such as decision making and economic theory, for example, as well as training each other in practical techniques. The project has spawned other rationality communities, including the Center for Applied Rationality, a non-profit organisation in Berkeley, California, which runs workshops on critical thinking and setting goals, and offline clubs, such as the New York City Sceptics and Bay Area Rationalist Community.". Lựa chọn H (theo bảng đáp án) là lựa chọn khớp với chi tiết này.
q34.evidence: Through this system on its website and forum, Less Wrong community members debate concepts such as decision making and economic theory, for example, as well as training each other in practical techniques. The project has spawned other rationality communities, including the Center for Applied Rationality, a non-profit organisation in Berkeley, California, which runs workshops on critical thinking and setting goals, and offline clubs, such as the New York City Sceptics and Bay Area Rationalist Community._

q36.explanation: Câu 36 yêu cầu nối thông tin trong câu hỏi với một lựa chọn. Đoạn E chứa căn cứ: "Science writer David Berreby notes that people generally cannot tell when they are being rational, cannot become more so by willpower alone and often believe their behaviour is rational when, in fact, it is not. He raises the example of court judges, in other words those who society entrusts with the responsibility to exercise power over others in order to enact the law. In a research study conducted by German psychologists Englich, Mussweiler and Strack, it was found that judges who rolled a die before adjudicating a case would give a longer sentence if a higher number came up than those who rolled a lower number.". Lựa chọn G (theo bảng đáp án) là lựa chọn khớp với chi tiết này.
q36.evidence: Science writer David Berreby notes that people generally cannot tell when they are being rational, cannot become more so by willpower alone and often believe their behaviour is rational when, in fact, it is not. He raises the example of court judges, in other words those who society entrusts with the responsibility to exercise power over others in order to enact the law. In a research study conducted by German psychologists Englich, Mussweiler and Strack, it was found that judges who rolled a die before adjudicating a case would give a longer sentence if a higher number came up than those who rolled a lower number._
